title: Motion Paths, Magnifying Docks, Live Highlights & Native Selects
description: Five real 2026 web-platform drops: curved motion-path animation, a magnifying macOS dock, the Custom Highlight API, Interest Invoker popovers, and native customizable selects.

drops.title: Motion-Path Thumbnail Flow
drops.description: A stacked deck of thumbnails unfurls along a curved CSS offset-path, each on its own staggered clock.
drops.concept: GSAP's MotionPath plugin — animating layered elements along a curved SVG trajectory — is trending again via a June 2026 Codrops tutorial that unfurls stacked thumbnails into a flowing strip. The same choreography is rebuilt here with native CSS offset-path and offset-distance, so the curve itself is a real animatable custom property instead of a JS tween library.
drops.source: Iqbal Muthahhary — Codrops "Creating a Thumbnail Flow Animation with GSAP MotionPath"

interaction.mouse: Hover a thumbnail mid-flight; click Unfurl to replay the sequence
interaction.click: Switch curve shape, stagger and speed in the tuning panel
interaction.touch: Tap Unfurl / Restack; tap a curve option to change the path

drops.title: Magnifying Dock Layout
drops.description: A macOS-style dock where icons scale by live pointer proximity, plus a click-to-fan icon stack.
drops.concept: Pure-CSS macOS dock recreations (like the docklr-css library) are a recurring layout trick for turning a flat icon row into a tactile, physically-responsive shelf. This drop keeps the no-JS dock's structure but adds a continuous JS proximity curve for the smooth Apple-style magnification a hover-only CSS selector can't achieve, plus a horizontal, vertical and "Stacks"-style variant.
drops.source: faisalman/docklr-css — "Give your site a macOS-like Dock menu & stacks using pure CSS3"

interaction.mouse: Move across a dock to see neighboring icons magnify; click the stack to fan it open
interaction.click: Adjust max scale and falloff radius in the tuning panel
interaction.touch: Drag a finger across a dock; tap the stack to expand/collapse

drops.title: Highlight API Text Painting
drops.description: Search, sentiment and reading-progress highlights painted onto text via Range objects, no re-rendered DOM.
drops.concept: The CSS Custom Highlight API reached full cross-engine support after Firefox 149 shipped it in March 2026, and 2026 write-ups are showcasing it for live search matching, AI sentiment heatmaps and proofreading — all without wrapping text in spans. This drop rebuilds three of those use cases with real Range/Highlight objects registered through CSS.highlights and styled purely via ::highlight().
drops.source: Kyoko Kitagawa — ICS MEDIA "CSS Custom Highlight API"

interaction.mouse: Type in the search box; drag the reading scrubber
interaction.click: Press Play to auto-scrub the reading-progress highlight
interaction.touch: Tap into the search field; drag the scrub slider with a finger

drops.title: Interest Invoker Reveals
drops.description: Tooltips, hover cards and row quick-actions triggered by declarative hover/focus "interest", not manual JS.
drops.concept: The Interest Invoker API's interestfor attribute and :interest-source/:interest-target pseudo-classes (refined on MDN through late 2025 into 2026) let hover-triggered popovers, tooltips and hover cards be wired in plain HTML instead of mouseenter/mouseleave listeners — the same declarative shift popover and dialog got a few years earlier, now applied to "showing on intent."
drops.source: MDN Web Docs — "Using interest invokers" (Popover API / Open UI Interest Invoker explainer)

interaction.mouse: Hover an icon, the profile link, or a row's ⋯ button
interaction.click: Tune show/hide delay in the panel; click a quick-action icon
interaction.touch: Long-press or tap-and-hold a trigger; tap elsewhere to dismiss

drops.title: Native Customizable Selects
drops.description: Real <select> menus with flags, avatars and swatches inside options — styled via appearance: base-select.
drops.concept: appearance: base-select, <selectedcontent> and ::picker(select) turn the plain native <select> into a fully brandable dropdown that still keeps built-in keyboard navigation, typeahead and form semantics — the exact feature a wave of March–April 2026 articles is pitching as the end of JS dropdown libraries like Select2 or react-select.
drops.source: Naeem Noor — modern.css "Customizable CSS Select with appearance: base-select"

interaction.mouse: Click a select to open its picker; hover rich options
interaction.click: Choose a country, assignee or theme option
interaction.touch: Tap to open the picker, tap an option to select it
